review_text:
I truly dislike leaving feedback that isn’t entirely positive, and it’s rare that I do so. However, after our recent stay, I feel it’s important to share our experience to help future guests and provide the management with an opportunity to address some maintenance and service concerns.

The Positives: 😊
There were several aspects of our stay that we genuinely enjoyed. The beds and pillows were exceptionally comfortable, which made for a great night's sleep. The shower had excellent water pressure and plenty of hot water, and the inclusion of quality toiletries was a lovely touch. We also appreciated the well-equipped kitchen, the full-sized fridge, and the effective air conditioning which kept us warm during the cool nights. The park facilities, particularly the clean pools and the communal fire pit area, are great assets to the property and the wildlife was a delight.

Areas for Improvement: 😞
While the cabin is advertised as having "extra space," we found it quite tight for four adults. The secondary bedroom is very small with minimal storage, (no storage in 2nd room, limited in main bedroom) meaning most of our group had to live out of suitcases on the floor. We also encountered a few maintenance and cleanliness issues:

Maintenance: Both bedroom sliding doors would stick if closed fully, requiring significant effort to reopen again. Additionally, the free Wi-Fi unfortunately did not connect/work at any point during our stay.

Cleanliness: While the bedrooms were tidy, the hard floors were quite dirty upon arrival—so much so that the bathmat turned brown immediately. The portable stovetop also required a good hard scrub to remove the oily surface before we could use it. We attempted to clean the floors ourselves, but the mop provided was in such poor condition and there was no dustpan available.

Amenities & Service: We were initially provided with only three towels & 1 washer for four adults. When we requested fresh towels after three days, we were disappointed by the reluctance by the female staff to assist. Being told that accessing the storage room was an inconvenience, as she would have to walk over and enter the code, felt like a lapse in basic customer service.

A Note on Checkout:
Our most significant concern was our departure. As we were packing the car, a member of the management team stood in a nearby doorway and watched us for nearly 10 minutes with his arms folded (while the other female from the office and the cleaning lady were inside that cabin). This felt quite intimidating and made our group feel very uncomfortable during our final moments at the park.

Summary:
Overall, this is a good property for short visits and the sleeping arrangements are excellent. We hope this feedback is taken in the constructive spirit intended, as a few small adjustments to cleaning protocols and staff interactions would truly elevate the guest experience. 💜
Cabin 102 was grate but there was some loose tiles in the bathroom
friendly staff

When we arrived the place looked good smelt clean
Then....
The bathroom shower you need a full hour to get wet and then the whole floor is just covered in water need to use all towels to soak water up hazard at its best but this is where it get bad
Spiders 🕷 🕷 the place is just filled with little spiders
Parnter and me was laying in bed trying to sleep where then I was bitten not once but 2 times I let the staff no on Saturday when we got back from activities then again was bitten on Saturday night lucky it wasn't the kids that got bitten
Was even a spider on the toilet paper roll lol
Cabin #11
Definitely for the price we payed which was over the top not impressed

Staying at Ingenia Cessnock was a wonderful surprise. Located on the doorstep of the vineyards we couldn’t have asked for a more convenient spot. Our rooms in the two bedroom villa were more than comfortable. There was plenty of space for 2 couples with a completely decked out kitchen with all mod cons. It was spotless and we were impressed with our view of and proximity to the pools, one of which was heated adding to its appeal coming into autumn. Staff were accommodating  and pleasant to deal with. Highly recommend it and will definitely return ..
For a 1 night stay.  You can't beat the price for a 2 bedroom cabin. Spacious,  clean, well equipped and quiet park.

Had a 2 night cabin stay at Ingenia and I must say the facilities are great.  Staff were very helpful as I was coming in after hours and left great instructions.  Bed was extremely comfortable which for me made my stay even better.  Camp kitchen is well equipped with BBQ's, hot plates and even a pizza oven.  Great playground for kids as well.  I would definitely stay again.
Good for 2 night affordable stay with fury member.

I travel alot for work and choose to stay in Ingenia parks when I can.
They always clean, comfy and spacious.
I travel with my little dog and love that Hunter Ingenia made sure she was comfortable during our stay by providing a nice big soft, clean bed for her and a bowl.
Thankyou for thinking of my little girls comfort as well as mine!

We stayed in cabin 102. Very spacious and comfortable. The 2 pools were fantastic. There were 23 in our group, mix of cabins, camping and caravan. It was 40 degrees and we loved in the pools. Staff lovely and helpful. Poolside sausage sizzle and pool games for Aussie day. We have a brilliant long weekend.

We had an unplanned visit here yesterday - car troubles on the way to Tamworth. The park could only accommodate us for one night, fair enough in school holidays.

Our car wasn't ready by checkout so the grounds keeper Brad, towed us to the back. The park staff gave us codes and access to amenities while we waited. We have three young children and nowhere to go while we waited for Cessnock Toyota (also amazing). Could not have been more helpful, friendly and accommodating.

We will definitely be back to visit. The pools, amenities, jumping pillow and adjoining Cafe were clean and new.

Kids saying they're happy the car broke down so that we could stay there is a great review in itself. Thanks again everyone at Cessnock Ingenia!
Amazing place to stay, great pools and amazing service

We booked a powered site for 2 nights in the heatwave. The artificial grass at the site made it really clean. The pool area (2 pools!) were a godsend. The camp kitchen and bathroom and laundry facilities were great.

Okay, I’m going to go in-depth because this place deserves a proper review.
The place itself is lovely — so lovely it has two pools, which is great because there are no beaches nearby. Plenty of options, nice vibes. We booked a budget cabin, and let me be clear… it is very much a budget cabin 😂
From the front it looks all cute and charming. The price was great for a last-minute stay and it’s puppy-friendly, which immediately won points. BUT the paint job inside absolutely sent me. It looks like everything was coated in undercoat and then… enthusiasm. Paint everywhere. I genuinely believe my chihuahua could have done a cleaner job with a tiny roller.
Now the real issue: the bathroom. This thing is a hazard zone. There’s no shower guard, no drainage outside the shower, and when you use it the entire bathroom turns into a slip-n-slide. I nearly went ass over head — which is not ideal when you’re pregnant. Olympic-level balance required.
I also forgot to mention the kitchen paint job… but honestly, the paint wasn’t the deal breaker. The bathroom situation is genuinely risky and really needs fixing before someone hurts themselves.
On the upside — the staff are AMAZING. Truly lovely humans. One beautiful staff member noticed towels hanging and brought fresh ones without even being asked. The front desk staff gave me candy canes and my dog treats, which resulted in a very happy adult/child (and dog).
Overall: great staff, nice facilities, budget cabin lives up to its name — but please fix the bathroom before someone needs a life alert.

Idéalement situé près de Pokolbin, vallée réputée de vins australiens.
Deux bémols cependant : pas d'emplacement à l'ombre, du moins sur ce que nous avons vu, et certains très proches d'une route très passante...
D'autres, plus éloignés et disponibles, étrangement, ne nous ont pas été proposés...rentrant hors des horaires d'ouverture, impossible de changer...
Pour le reste, rien à redire...
Hele fijne plek in het centrum van de Hunter Valley! Vriendelijk en behulpzaam personeel! Heerlijk zwembad met jacuzzi. Je camper staat tussen de wijnstruiken😀
Great cafe
Excellent food
Lovely holiday park, easy communication, level site for motorhome with lovely imitation grass at the side, very clean amenities, lovely roses in gardens.

Used washing machines on-site to launder our very expensive riding shirts for the Black Dog Ride and they all came out stained and ruined. With what looks like oil or grease stains.
WiFi is absolutely terrible.
Nice and basic.

As cabins go in caravan parks the Villa we stayed in has been the best one so far. Right on the pool. Extras amazing eg TV and air conditioning in all bedrooms, picnic crockery and cutlery and plenty of ordinary kitchen utensils etc.

On the first night, a group of guests made loud noises until midnight, which was terrible. After reporting it to management, they kind of improved a bit for the following nights. Despite this, everything else was perfect. The staff were extremely nice and helpful, the bathrooms were very clean, and the amenities were very close and easy to access.
I liked the pool’s ,very fun great for the kids. Staff was amazing very helpful and nice and facilities was good aswell. Overall a fun clean nice stay.

Excellent, efficient isn't booking and late arrival process! Lovely 2 bedroom cabin number 8. Beautiful queen bed, great location,
but if Mum and Dad want some privacy, they need to close the door but the air conditioning is up the other end of the cabin!! There is a pedestal fan but that's not much good in winter!! Good sized fridge, kitchen is provisioned with all you need to cook for 4. Leafy position, access to the camp kitchen, complete with wood fired pizza oven and open fire! Great value, spacious, only the bathroom is quite small, and no place to hang towels for 4.

Great place to stay. I was disappointed at check in as we timed our trip to be able to check in at 2pm. On arrival we were told to go away and come back in 40 minutes. My question was to go where? To which I got a blank stare. Luckily a fellow guest told me there was coffee shop next door where we could sit and wait. I think your check in staff need some training. By contrast your check out staff were terrific.
Spent a weekend here attending a wedding ceremony locally.  Great central location for a base to explore the wineries and restaurants. Super friendly staff with local knowledge.  Great weekend. 👍
Stayed in a caravan on a powered site. Good location and great amenities. Pool was heated which the kids loved.
Very comfortable bed, great air conditioner and super clean. Will definitely use them again.

We cant recommend this place any more. This was our first time staying in a holiday park and We weren't expecting much but it far exceeded our expectations. The villa's are clean and modern and have all the amenities we require. There is so much for the kids to do here. We will definitely stay here again

Stayed in a one room cabin, it was basic clean, but the tops of the shelves, top of microwave were very dirty. The cups, plates and cutlery were sticking so we had to wash them all. The blankets were full of dust and the room was not vacuum properly. The BBQ area is great with a fire going most days and a book swap shelf

Not great unless you like lots of screaming kids (yes it's the last night of the school hols!) whose parents don't care that they ride their scooters in the camp kitchen. Amenities get messy and need checking more often especially for toilet paper. These places are great for kids though. We're happy to go elsewhere next time.
Well maintained site and very convenient location. Kids friendly and the fire pit is lovely.
It was very clean, neat and tidy. Would def come back again.
因為要坐熱氣球的緣故 搵間近HV嘅地方住宿 似度假村 沒有善用設施 但職員溝通友善 努力協調晚歸早出的我們😅謝謝🙏🏻另外可以留意一下 風筒放在水喉下面櫃內 濕唒 用唔到啊

We only stayed overnight as we were picking up our new puppy, but I'd have liked to stay longer. The staff were were helpful and kind. The park was lovely and quiet. The cabin was very clean and roomy. The shower had excellent pressure and the bed and pillows were very comfortable, we had a great night sleep. Love that it is pet friendly too.
Thank you for such an enjoyable stay.

For my sons 31st birthday in June (so the weather was quite cold) I had booked 2 x 2 bedroom cabins for my family (6 adults and four young grandchildren ages all from 3 and under) to stay in while attending the park from Thursday to Monday morning for a great family gathering for a few days.  When I booked i had requested the 2 x 2 bedroom cabins be close to each other to accommodate my families needs.  I then also booked the day before we were due to arrive another cabin which was called a "budget cabin".  In total I had booked 3 cabins only to find out on arrival that one of the 2 bedroom cabins was not available due to an issue (apparently) to do with a concern or something that wasn't appropriate for us to use.  We were informed by a female staff member we would be given an upgraded room which happened to be a cabin a bit bigger but was very close to the pool.  I believe they call it the "pool view cabin".   Due to the ages of my grandchildren and the cold weather my son and his wife were very disappointed with the cabin upgrade due to these issues.  My daughter in law and I went into the office to talk to the female staff member with whom we'd spoken to when we arrived.  We realised the 2 x 2 bedroom cabins were not very close really in proximity to each other at all.  One cabin was number 27 and the other was 102 very much apart from each other, so not very close at all.  We also made mention about the upgraded room being too close to the pool especially having two children 3 and 1.  My daughter in law didnt feel comfortable staying in the pool cabin the female staff member was very rude saying "nobody refuses an upgrade" and became even more rude, quite confronting, making very unrealistic comments to us very sarcastically saying "well we just can't pick up cabin 27 and put it closer to cabin  102. In the end we stayed in 2 x budget cabins and 1 x 2 bedroom cabin.  There was no apology no offer of any type of refund as I had paid well over $2,000. We then decided to enjoy our stayed irrespective of all the issues.
Great cabin stay  clean well equipped   Great camp kitchen beautiful pool kids play area. We enjoyed our stay plus dog friendly cabins

Nice staff and good cafe on site. Decent coffee 😀.
Close to plenty of the wineries 👍
Staff are really friendly and helpful, rooms are clean and tidy.

Absolutely amazing place ♥️
Very kind and supportive of our special needs lil family 💙💙💙💙♥️♥️♥️
Our 3rd stay in a last 12 months its that good

Great location and friendly service! We stayed for a night to head to the Mega Creatures exhibition at the Hunter Valley Gardens. Our cabin was clean and well kept plus we found some complimentary V in our fridge. Check in was easy and our host was lovely. Nice little spot for a young family!

Overall, good stay. There are 2 pools and one heated, a kids mini playground and jumping air platform for the kids. Staff were kind and helpful and even got a free bottle of wine.  There were some parts that weren't so great but you get what you pay for.

Drive through site was spacious and easy to access. Close to amenities block. Various activities for the children during easter, pedal karts, two swimming pools and inflatable trampoline. Close to Cessnock and wineries.
Our cabin was spacious, clean and comfortable. It was well equipped with everything we could need.
Nice place cabin a bit small on a main road bit noisy the food shop fantastic food best hamburger I have eaten in a long time staff very friendly
Staff were wonderful and our stay was excellent.
very clean and every stuff there, pan, BBQ grill, clean shower, pool, jaccuzi, kids playground.

This little caravan park was very clean and conveniently located for all our needs. We’ve travelled 1/2 way round Australia and were extremely impressed by the lovely accessories located in the main bathrooms but especially the disability bathroom. It felt very homely. Thanks for the comfort of home!

No car park outside of our cabin, we parked in a different location and have to walk all our belongings to site, also have 2x rooms one of the rooms is set up differently to the main and haven't received the same amenities as that room
Stayed in our Caravan for Day on the Green Lovely staff , good communal kitchen , clean showers , great Cafe & brilliant pool
Stayed here a couple of times now. Smaller park with good amenities and a couple of pools. Friendly and helpful staff. Dog friendly.
Nice caravan park. Great pool area, clean amenities.

Ingenia Holidays Hunter Valley was so much fun. Great for pet owners, parents or adults of all ages. You have a wide range of cabins, caravans or a huge powered sites for all you tent goers. Then they got it all. Plus a pool and playground for your little ones.

We were lucky enough to get a room upgrade to a villa and the room was excellent. Service was excellent and staff are very friendly and accommodating. Will definitely be back again. Thank you Ingenia!
We have stayed here before and love it. The kids love it! A great little park with an amazing pool and play area for the kids.

Customer service was awesome from the reception team. They were kind, accomodating and done their best to help us. Huge thank you to the women at reception.

Budget room for four people was clean and tidy! Nice size patio to relax. Two pools and a spa was great!

Only thing, when family got in the spa with silver jewellery it completely tarnished all of their jewellery and sheets smelt a bit musky.

Would absolutely return with our kids. Location is pretty good for concerts and close to town
Lovely caravan park, clean and tidy. We loved the pools and the rooms were very clean and comfortable. Friendly staff and it’s located close to shops, restaurants, clubs, wineries and much more.

One of the best Caravan Parks I've been to, the employees were super nice! Pools were very nice and they even have homes for people who don't have a caravan. Bugs were almost everywhere, but it wasn't their problem since the location was like more of a forest area. Otherwise, everything was splendid!

Tried to call multiple times leading up to our stay however no response.
Cabin was clean and comfortable. Pool was amazing. However the park isn't for us with the strictness of things.
Small luxury caravan park everything close by, spa pool with shade areas clean friendly and helpful staff even the wild life was friendly relax environment highly recommend

Booked 1br cabin got another cabin same size 2 bunk beds didn't want filthy sofa and a table only 1 could eat at due to no room
We looked in cabin next to us this was the one in photo I booked what did you give us
My entire family were pleasantly impressed by the cabin we stayed in. The facilities at the site and the service we received by the people who worked at the site.
We had a amazing time at this park the pool and grounds were great and the kids had a blast!

Edit:
3rd time here! The best so far.
Out go to for our stay at the hunter.
As for the complimentary V-cans and chocolates, honestly you guys rock!!!
We will be back every year.
Water pressure wasn’t great and fan was on backwards but everything else was lovely
Absolutely ideal. Fabulous facilities. The great for couples or families. An oasis. Lovely cabins. Spotless , self contained. Perfect. Great hospitality.Can't wait to stay again

Excellent 2 and 3 bedroom villas right next to the pool. Very family friendly. Excellent little pool that is not too crowded. In a central location near all the wineries and restaurants. Easy to get in. Cabins are modern and spacious with nice touches like beach towels coffee capsules and make up remover

We had a wonderful time staying at this cabin.The location was perfect,the cabin beautifully presented.
The fully equipped kitchen allowed us to cook meals easily.Will book it again.

The staff were friendly and accommodating. Everything was clean. Beds and couch were very comfortable. We were given complimentary V’s which was nice. We will definitely be back and recommend this place to all family and friends

This company practices price gouging when concerts are on in the area.It was impossible to drive thru the powered sites, even though we paid extra to do so, as cars have to park outside their van spaces and the streets are too narrow

This place is really good 😍
The cabin was nice, very clean and the pool is huge with lots of seating. The staff were really nice too.
Nice little cafe on site with great food 😋

This facility is ideal for access to the Wineries and the Cessnock shopping center. It deserves a score above 4 stars for most users.
I booked for 5 days and was placed by the fence line adjacent to the main road which was very noisy with trucks and a nightly motorbike speed demon.
Main deficiency was that caravans backed into the fence had no place to park the towing vehicle except half way across the access road. Not safe, not acceptable.
If you need to drive through, insist on the center row of sites the you will have enough room to park across in front of your site.
Very clean, fun for kids, no complaints. I would stay again

Stayed in our caravan which was a big rig and they accommodated us for this. Staff were extremely helpful and friendly. Facilities were sooo clean and maintained. It was a quiet location and easily accessible to shops down the ride about 1.5kms
They catered to different needs perfectly. Pool area was beautiful and again kept spotless.
There is a cafe adjoined to the reception area which is very handy for a cuppa or burger. Lovely atmosphere.
Highly Recommend!
Very spacious, clean & well appointed. Staff are very helpful. I took my dog & I was thrilled that she was able to sleep inside with me.Thank you so much.
Great for the kids and central location for the town and tourist attractions.
Loved staying here - great options for travelling with friends or family!

Plans had to change from a powered sites to a cabin at last minute, luckily one left on a busy weekend. Maddy on reception  was so helpful and as our Van had to be towed and we had an event to go to she organised a Taxi, nothing was to much trouble and Cafe served a lovely Coffee, thank you.

Such a good value for money.

The best stuff!
Gave us a free bunk when a storm hit our tent.
Beautiful place and location for holiday loved it

Making the booking you deal with Teagan isor Maddy. Both are just bouncy helpful knowledgeable friendly people that will solve any issues you may have, not just on the phone but also during your visit.
So impressed.
We stayed in our caravan. Given a convenient drive through power site, close to amenities, loved patting my dog and made her welcome.
Great visit.

The location is very convenient, close drive to shops and not too far from Pokolbin or Lovedale. The staff were very friendly and extremely accommodating. It was a very chilled campsite a lot smaller than many other Ingenia's. The reason I gave 3 stars for the rooms is because we found the 2br budget cabin to be a bit outdated for the price. This park is on the expensive side due to the location and limited spots available.

Why even bother having wifi if it's this slow

I have been to lots of non western hotels and none of them run at 0.10mbps

Otherwise good place, nice pool, brilliant room, but I feel I need to get that off my chest

Hunter Valley is beautiful, I always used to stay at Ingenia. Until this last time. I searched for a pet friendly place and I got allegedly “upgraded” to a Villa. And I say “allegedly” because I paid 558 dollars for 2 nights, which is not the price for the cabins. I came with my pet and suddenly, at my second day, I was told I had to pay a fine of 100 dollars for having a dog in the Villa. Not to mention the receptionist basically called me a liar at my face. Never coming back. If you have dogs, stay away from this place.

EDIT: After your reply, I feel even further disrespected. Pull out the cam on my check-in and see myself informing your receptionist that I had a dog, while listing the guests after being informed we were not allowed to walk around the residence area (if I had kids).

The next day, I was awaken to be told dogs were not allowed at the Villas (which allegedly was an "UPGRADE") and that due to this reason they were applying a 100$ penalty on me, which I disagre, but paid for.

It also seems your employees need to follow their own policies. I respected every single one of them. Again, this is a complete mess generated by your own staff.

Additionally, your receptionist who received the information about the dog during check-in (again, PLEASE DO check the cam if you have one), denied it later the other day when applying the penalty (basically calling me a liar). So, no, thanks. I will never bring my pet or stay with you again.
We stayed here for three days . Overall an awesome experience just few things like bathroom door was not shutting properly. Also, it would be great if towels were replaced in the morning.

Had a great trip to Hunter Valley, and Ingenia provided the best value for our stay. The cabin had all the essentials, was comfortable, and quiet. The staff also recommended some good places to visit in the area.

Very old not like pictures on web site
Blinds didn’t shut out lights in bedroom

We were really excited to return for our second stay, but this time, we faced an unexpected situation.

At check-in, we informed the staff that we had a dog with us. They provided us with instructions on where we could and couldn't walk the dog, along with the cabin key.

The next day, however, we were surprised when someone knocked on our door, informing us that we would be charged an additional $100 for not mentioning the dog earlier at the check in.

Attached is a copy of the instructions we received, which clearly includes information about our dog.

What bothered us the most was the staff accusing us of lying.
Awesome! So clean! Facilities were great, close to everything and the ladies in the office are really friendly and helpful. Highly recommend :)

What can I say….. location, location, location….. smack bang in the middle of beautiful wineries and within walking distance to the centre of town! Staff are lovely and the camp kitchen with an open fireplace was very well equipped. Pretty pool and spa….. only downside was some of the sites are very small, I would suggest booking ahead if you have a large caravan or motorhome.

A very good holiday park! My family got to share a cabin, which was a decent size, bigger than the average hotel room. There were two beds, one in the bedroom and the other in the living room. It was VERY comfortable. The cabin was very well cleaned and provided things like soap, toilet paper, hair and body wash, and much more! There was a playground not far from our cabin, perfect for the kids! I would highly recommend this to any guest coming to Hunter Valley.
I tried to get some information first thing I figure out hiding prices

In April, I booked 2 powered sites for September, and asked for a quiet location, as we were coming from a busy location on the beachfront, and wanted some peace and quiet.. On arrival, we found that we were on sites 13 and 14, which were right next to Mount View road, one of the busiest roads in Cessnock as it leads to the wineries. It also seems to be a race track for all the hoons on bikes, and in hotted up cars. We pre paid $60 per night for our sites, only to discover that we could have paid $25 per night at the Cessnock showgrounds next door. If you absolutely have to stay at Ingenia Hunter Valley, do not accept sites 15, 14 ,13, or P5 to P10. We had to take sleeping pills, and wear ear plugs to get some sleep, and were often woken up by some idiot on a loud motorbike during the night.

We were in caravan.
Easy parking and great facilities

We had a pool side villa absolutely beautiful
Had everything you need. Aircon in all rooms
Television in two rooms. Also the Staff are very nice and friendly. The only negative comment I have is the  beds way to soft some people might like them like that, apart from that great park for Kids.
Very clean ammonites and probably one of the best Camp Kitchens. Would recommend.

This park is only a small park with roughly about 27 sites. The amenities are clean and tidy and they even supply bath mats for the shower floor.  There is also a communal fire pit with wood and kindling supplied Friday and Saturday nights, a pizza oven with utensils available from the office and 2 large pools plus heaps more in this tiny park. The staff are lovely and are available to help in any way necessary. Definitely coming back again soon!

We had a great stay for one night here. The kitchen was clean and well laid out. The amenities were also good as well. Friendly staff on arrival. Only downside was the WiFi didn’t work at all but wasn’t too much of an issue. Would recommend staying here if you’re in Cessnock
Nice location… very noisy park.
Located near the heart of the city, has the basics + kitchen and washing machine + dryer. You can use the park facilities too! Can smell abit funky due to previous guests cooking in the space

Booked 2 nights on an powered site during the school holidays and had to cancel 5 days prior and got changed a $50 cancellation fee! 1 night is worth $89 so that is near 40% of that total cost of stay I had to pay to cancel! What a rort! I would understand if it were within 24hrs but 100% sure they would fill that spot within 5 days during peak school holidays! Absolutely ridiculous!!

We enjoyed our stay at the park,the staff are friendly and pleasant.The facilities are excellent.Where we were situated was unfortunately tight and no where to park your car safely.Overall a pleasant park and clean.

The location is the best place to stay as it is closed to vineyards and cellar doors - Saddler’s creek, Savanna’s vineyard and Iron gate . The camp kitchen is good with open wood  fire heater and TV. The amenities are good and all you need. I liked the outdoor fire pit, the woods have been supplied by the park. The power site is good with the astro turf , nice and clean. This is our second visit and we will definitely return for the future trip .
Nice position, good facilities & friendly service.

Nice little holiday park.
A vast majority looks to have been converted into private residences, so the holiday rental section of the park is only very small. This being said you have everything you need; the cabins are well equipped and there is also a huge camp kitchen and toilet block. There is also a heated pool and a bouncy pillow for the kids. The ground is nice and flat so it’s perfect to bring bikes along for the kids to ride around if the weather is nice.
Our only qualm was that our cabin was a fair distance from our parking spot, which was extremely inconvenient with a toddler, a baby & our luggage in the rain. The direct path from our parking spot to our cabin was roped off, so we had to walk around the park to get from one to the other which left us and our belongings soaked. It looks like there had previously been a parking spot directly alongside our cabin, however when the back- section of the park was converted into private residences they’d fenced off access to the parking spot, so now you have to park over by the pool instead of alongside your cabin.
If you have children or mobility issues, I’d recommend asking for a cabin close to your parking spot (aka not cabin 10 or 11) to avoid the inconvenience.
Close to Wineries great amenities

The only downside is that they advertise a restaurant/cafe on the premises. This is not true.
Lovely showers, pool clean.
Basic rooms but they have what you need. There is a large central kitchen next to it
We booked a budget cabin, nice room with cooking equipment
Good spot for holidays, Good place for caravan park..
Showers excellent, lovely surroundings, plenty of room to reverse our 21 foot caravans as we missed out on getting a drive through.

Absolutely loved our stay here! It’s a sleepy little site and the facilities are great. Two amazing pools, great camp kitchen, super safe for kids to zoom on their bikes, and dog friendly! Very close to amenities.

I stayed in a budget cabin and it was okay but was infested with German cockroaches, when I would go out of a night and come back and turn the light on heaps of cockroaches would scatter all over the lounge room/kitchen area. Apart from that staff were lovely.

We had a powered sight for our van,very clean.Saff were very friendly and when we found ourselves locked in our van due to a malfuntioning door staff were quickly there to let us out.Will go back there again when visiting family in the future..highly recommend this place..😊
Nice little caravan park with pool, hot tub, mini playground and jumping pillow.
Enjoyed staying with you

So.. this was our first trip with our camper trailer, and it was just so relaxing!|Stayed for 2 nights, mid week, it was great.|Staff were so great upon check-in and check out. Amenities were clean and pretty central.|The pool was the best part, so good after a hot and sticky travel. The Caravan Park was so relaxing and inviting. It's so good to get around so casually. It's definitely in a good spot. It wasn't too busy (at least where we were in the park).|The Hunter Valley is so gorgeous, cessnock people are happy and friendly.|Will definitely be returning.
Great! The cabins were spacious and had everything we needed. Great location. Will definitely stay again

We stayed here for 3 nights and it was great.
Amenities are very clean. Fantastic camp kitchen. There's even a book swap.
Pool and heated spa were excellent.
Highly recommend.
Special thanks to Helen for making check in easy and fun.
We will definitely be back.
We enjoyed our stay at this park: sites were clean (artificial grass), pools were excellent, service friendly.
Clean and comfortable.
Lovely pool facility. Rooms were family friendly
It's a well located site for going to vineyards, good camp kitchen not much around.
Absolutely gorgeous place to stay well. Clean looked after pool. Great access. Great little spot go check it out
Nice central place to stay. A smaller park so therefore the sites are smaller unless you have a drive through site. Staff are nice and amenities good.

Was there for the weekend for a wedding,
The cabin was that small you couldn't swing a cat in it,
The floor was filthy broken plate under the lounge. The floor looked like I has never been mopped.
Some sort of stain on the lounge it was not nice at all something on the table leg like blood 🤢🤢
The cracked roof the list went on and on.

Don't waste your money.

I did go to your receptionist and unfortunately was no help at all,
Was told you are fully booked and that was it didn't really have any care factor at all.

I loved it but the issues were the water pressure, the laundry in my villa smelt like sewage and a few of the towels had stains but pleaseee let me know what mattress you guys use please please please  also I stayed in the vila 2 and it was 2 story 6 people by the pool nice sunset

As a wheelchair user myself, I would highly recommend the 1 bedroom cabin we stayed in  for those wanting to stay in the Hunter area. As  an added bonus, my Assistance Dog really enjoyed  her walks around the park which has beautifully maintained grounds. Not being able able to access Reception was a minor nuisance. Even my husband enjoyed his stay!
Good cabin and good price. 👍

Staying at a powered caravan site we were very comfortable. The facilities were all beautifully kept, clean, tidy and modern. The pool was great too. The encounters we had with staff were friendly and efficient. The only difficulty is that access to the site was a little cramped; not too bad with our small 14' 'van and a clear site in front to utilise as well as the road but, longer vans had to manoeuvre a lot. the camp kitchen was lovely and convenient.
Good job a beautiful place.

This is a really small caravan spot, but we were lucky to get an upgrade with a cabin right in front of the pool, which was amazing. The room itself was spotless, couldn't be happier with the cabin, we wanted to live there. A few small things like a light blown in the kitchen and tv not working in the main bedroom, but these things were incidental when you have 2 great pools to choose from, a heated spa, a jumping pillow and mega chess set and connect 4 game....and go kart style bikes you can hire for the kids. My kids had the best time ever, and we will definitely be back.
Bruits voitures et motos et chiens qui aboient.Pas de wifi. Pas de présentation du bungalow. Pas assez de vaisselles pour cuisiner. Il fait froid la nuit.
Confortable and easy to access
4 kids Mum & Dad. Stayed 3 nights, great park, safe for the kids to ride around on there bikes. No complaints. The staff were friendly but none intrusive. Just perfect really.
Welcoming staff and well-maintained facilities with very clean rooms.

We recently stayed here on our trip to hunter valley. We were in cabin 27 and unfortunately it was not a pleasant experience. The street currently has road works which we were not prepared about. The cabin faces the road where temporary lights are set up and the traffic noise woke us at different hours of the night. The care takers lodge is next to the cabin and their dogs barked incesently in the early morning waking us again. The single beds in bed 2 were nothing more then a bed base with worn down foam/blankets on top which was ok for our 3 year old but for a older children or adult would be extremely uncomfortable. The screen door would not be able to be utilised in warmer weathers if you have young children as it does not shut properly or lock. For the price we paid I would have much preferred to stay at Rydges in Pokolbin or another nice estate.
Perfect place to stay with kids, nice and quiet. The rooms are clean and spacious, and only a short drive away from the wine regions.
Very Nice & Calm Resort, near to all ameneties.
Really good location, staff were very helpful. Great place for my partner and our dog last minute. the bed in the cabin is really good.

I already did in a survey, but would highly recommend as cabins were great everything was clean, the facilities were great and the location.
Thanks Sheryl.
Lovely clean and safe park, well appointed camp kitchen

Excellent camp kitchen. Open fire and pizza oven . |Small park but clean and tidy .|Toilets and showers excellent.|Road noise not too bad .|Dog friendly .|Snacks available|Lovely pool and spa when its warm enough.
